reasoning: The current option-adjusted spread (as of Apr 11) is 0.99, driven by a sharp spike from ~0.76 in March due to new massive 10% baseline tariffs and 104% tariffs on China effective April 5-9. However, the spread plateaued and dipped slightly (1.02 on Apr 9 to 0.99 by Apr 11), suggesting the immediate panic is priced in and some mean-reversion is beginning. High-quality IG bonds often see demand after selloffs as investors "buy the dip" and seek safety. The FRED combination tool indicates a 43% chance of being >0.99 by April 20. Accordingly, my near-term forecast favors a slight tightening or stabilization (P < 0.5). To reflect increasing uncertainty over time per the instructions, the probabilities fade toward 0.50 for the longer-term resolution dates.
